heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by William Wyler. Written by Karl Tunberg, based on the novel by Lew Wallace.
clean: Directed by William Wyler. Written by Karl Tunberg, based on the novel by Lew Wallace.

heading: Judah Ben-Hur
raw: You may conquer the land, you may slaughter the people. That is not the end. We will rise again.
clean: You may conquer the land, you may slaughter the people. That is not the end. We will rise again.

heading: Judah Ben-Hur
raw: Rome is an affront to God. Rome is strangling my people and my country and the whole earth, but not forever. I tell you, the day Rome falls, there will be a shout of freedom such as the world has never heard before.
clean: Rome is an affront to God. Rome is strangling my people and my country and the whole earth, but not forever. I tell you, the day Rome falls, there will be a shout of freedom such as the world has never heard before.

heading: Judah Ben-Hur
raw: [to Messala] May God grant me vengeance. I pray that you live till I return.
clean: May God grant me vengeance. I pray that you live till I return.

heading: Judah Ben-Hur
raw: Find them, Messala. Restore them to me and I'll forget what I vowed with every stroke of that oar you chained me to.
clean: Find them, Messala. Restore them to me and I'll forget what I vowed with every stroke of that oar you chained me to.

heading: Messala
raw: It's an insane world, but in it there's one sanity, the loyalty of old friends. Judah, we must believe in one another.
clean: It's an insane world, but in it there's one sanity, the loyalty of old friends. Judah, we must believe in one another.

heading: Messala
raw: [to Judah] I wanted your help. Now you have given it to me. By making this example of you, I discourage treason. By condemning, without hesitation, an old friend, I shall be feared.
clean: I wanted your help. Now you have given it to me. By making this example of you, I discourage treason. By condemning, without hesitation, an old friend, I shall be feared.

heading: Quintus Arrius
raw: [to Judah] You have the spirit to fight back but the good sense to control it. Your eyes are full of hate, Forty-One. That's good. Hate keeps a man alive. It gives him strength.
clean: You have the spirit to fight back but the good sense to control it. Your eyes are full of hate, Forty-One. That's good. Hate keeps a man alive. It gives him strength.

heading: Quintus Arrius
raw: [to Judah] In his eagerness to save you, your God has also saved the Roman fleet.
clean: In his eagerness to save you, your God has also saved the Roman fleet.

heading: Sheik Ilderim
raw: One wife? One God, that I can understand - but one wife! That is not civilized. It is not generous.
clean: One wife? One God, that I can understand - but one wife! That is not civilized. It is not generous.

heading: Sheik Ilderim
raw: [to Judah] The Star of David, will shine out for your people and my people together and blind the eyes of Rome.
clean: The Star of David, will shine out for your people and my people together and blind the eyes of Rome.

heading: Others
raw: Sextus: There's also a wild man in the desert named John the Baptist and a young carpenter's son who goes around doing magic tricks, miracles they call them...This man is different. He teaches that God is near, in every man. It's actually quite profound, some of it.
clean: There's also a wild man in the desert named John the Baptist and a young carpenter's son who goes around doing magic tricks, miracles they call them...This man is different. He teaches that God is near, in every man. It's actually quite profound, some of it.

heading: Others
raw: Esther: I've seen too much what hate can do. My father is burned up with it. But I've heard of a young rabbi who says that forgiveness is greater and love more powerful than hatred. I believe it.
clean: I've seen too much what hate can do. My father is burned up with it. But I've heard of a young rabbi who says that forgiveness is greater and love more powerful than hatred. I believe it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Sextus: You can break a man's skull. You can arrest him. You can throw him into a dungeon. But how do you fight an idea?
clean: You can break a man's skull. You can arrest him. You can throw him into a dungeon. But how do you fight an idea?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Messala: Sextus, you ask how to fight an idea. Well, I'll tell you how... with another idea!
clean: Sextus, you ask how to fight an idea. Well, I'll tell you how... with another idea!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: I would do anything for you, Messala... except betray my own people.
clean: I would do anything for you, Messala... except betray my own people.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Messala: In the name of all the gods, Judah, what do the lives of a few Jews mean to you?
clean: In the name of all the gods, Judah, what do the lives of a few Jews mean to you?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: If I cannot persuade them, that does not mean I will help you... murder them. Besides, you must understand this, Messala. I believe in the past of my people, and in their future.
clean: If I cannot persuade them, that does not mean I will help you... murder them. Besides, you must understand this, Messala. I believe in the past of my people, and in their future.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Messala: Future? You are a conquered people.
clean: Future? You are a conquered people.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: You may conquer the land; you may slaughter the people. But that is not the end; we will rise again.
clean: You may conquer the land; you may slaughter the people. But that is not the end; we will rise again.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Messala: You live on dead dreams; you live on the myths of the past. The glory of Solomon is gone and you think it will return?. Joshua will not rise again to save you nor David. There is only one reality in the world today. Look to the West, Judah! Don't be a fool, look to Rome!
clean: You live on dead dreams; you live on the myths of the past. The glory of Solomon is gone and you think it will return?. Joshua will not rise again to save you nor David. There is only one reality in the world today. Look to the West, Judah! Don't be a fool, look to Rome!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: I would rather be a fool than a traitor... or a killer!
clean: I would rather be a fool than a traitor... or a killer!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Messala: I am a soldier!
clean: I am a soldier!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: Yes! Who kills! For Rome! And Rome is evil!
clean: Yes! Who kills! For Rome! And Rome is evil!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Messala: I warn you...
clean: I warn you...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: No! I warn you! Rome is an affront to God! Rome is strangling my people and my country, the whole Earth! But not forever. And I tell you the day Rome falls there will be a shout of freedom such as the world has never heard before!
clean: No! I warn you! Rome is an affront to God! Rome is strangling my people and my country, the whole Earth! But not forever. And I tell you the day Rome falls there will be a shout of freedom such as the world has never heard before!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Messala: Judah, either you help me or you oppose me, you have no other choice. You're either for me or against me!
clean: Judah, either you help me or you oppose me, you have no other choice. You're either for me or against me!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: If that is the choice, then I am against you.
clean: If that is the choice, then I am against you.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: If you were not a bride I would kiss you goodbye
clean: If you were not a bride I would kiss you goodbye

heading: Dialogue
raw: Esther: If I were not a bride there would be no goodbyes to be said.
clean: If I were not a bride there would be no goodbyes to be said.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Arrius: What service have you seen? Forty-One?
clean: What service have you seen? Forty-One?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: A month's less a day in this ship.
clean: A month's less a day in this ship.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Arrius: You keep an exact account. And before?
clean: You keep an exact account. And before?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: Three years in other ships.
clean: Three years in other ships.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Arrius: What is your name, Forty-One?
clean: What is your name, Forty-One?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: [pause] Judah Ben-Hur.
clean: Judah Ben-Hur.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Arrius: Judah Ben-Hur... Let me die.
clean: Judah Ben-Hur... Let me die.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: [repeating Arrius' words on board the galleys] We keep you alive to serve this ship. Row well and live.
clean: We keep you alive to serve this ship. Row well and live.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: It's a strange destiny that brought me to a new life... a new home... a new father. It brought me here. It may take me away. But wherever I may be... I shall always try to wear this ring as a son of Arrius should. With gratitude and affection... and with honor.
clean: It's a strange destiny that brought me to a new life... a new home... a new father. It brought me here. It may take me away. But wherever I may be... I shall always try to wear this ring as a son of Arrius should. With gratitude and affection... and with honor.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Arrius: You're leaving.
clean: You're leaving.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: I must.
clean: I must.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Arrius: It's a journey I can't keep you from. Your eyes have never left it all these months. I can recognize pain. But still, my counsel is to wait. Gratus is to be replaced. The governorship goes to Pontius Pilate.
clean: It's a journey I can't keep you from. Your eyes have never left it all these months. I can recognize pain. But still, my counsel is to wait. Gratus is to be replaced. The governorship goes to Pontius Pilate.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: When?
clean: When?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Arrius: Very soon. It's very much to our purpose.
clean: Very soon. It's very much to our purpose.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: Nothing is to the purpose if, when I come back to Judea, I come too late. That is the thought which has punished me every moment I've spent here.
clean: Nothing is to the purpose if, when I come back to Judea, I come too late. That is the thought which has punished me every moment I've spent here.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Arrius: Will Rome see you again?
clean: Will Rome see you again?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: Here is a part of my life which you have made for me. I shall remember it.
clean: Here is a part of my life which you have made for me. I shall remember it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Arrius: Whoever the gods are, they take small interest in an old man's hopes.
clean: Whoever the gods are, they take small interest in an old man's hopes.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: They have their way.
clean: They have their way.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: I must deal with Messala in my own way.
clean: I must deal with Messala in my own way.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Balthasar: And your way is to kill him. I see this terrible thing in your eyes, Judah Ben-Hur. But no matter what this man has done to you, you have no right to take his life. He will be punished inevitably.
clean: And your way is to kill him. I see this terrible thing in your eyes, Judah Ben-Hur. But no matter what this man has done to you, you have no right to take his life. He will be punished inevitably.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: I don't believe in miracles.
clean: I don't believe in miracles.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Messala: Triumph complete, Judah. The race won. The enemy destroyed.
clean: Triumph complete, Judah. The race won. The enemy destroyed.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: I see no enemy.
clean: I see no enemy.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Messala: What do you think you see? The smashed body of a wretched animal! Is enough of a man still left here for you to hate? Let me help you... [convulses with pain] You think they're dead. Your mother and sister. Dead. And the race over. It isn't over, Judah. They're not dead.
clean: What do you think you see? The smashed body of a wretched animal! Is enough of a man still left here for you to hate? Let me help you... You think they're dead. Your mother and sister. Dead. And the race over. It isn't over, Judah. They're not dead.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: Where are they? Where are they? WHERE ARE THEY?!
clean: Where are they? Where are they? WHERE ARE THEY?!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Messala: [last words] Look for them in the Valley of the Lepers, if you can recognize them. [Judah doubles over with grief] It goes on. It goes on, Judah. The race... the race is not over! [dies]
clean: Look for them in the Valley of the Lepers, if you can recognize them. It goes on. It goes on, Judah. The race... the race is not over!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: I am Judah Ben-Hur.
clean: I am Judah Ben-Hur.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: I'm already part of this tragedy.
clean: I'm already part of this tragedy.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Esther: [hugs Judah] Oh, Judah. I was afraid you would never come back... afraid of what would happen to you. But I see you again.
clean: Oh, Judah. I was afraid you would never come back... afraid of what would happen to you. But I see you again.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: Your father's waiting for me. Where is he?
clean: Your father's waiting for me. Where is he?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Esther: What do you mean to do? [Judah does not answer] Oh, Judah. Rest, sleep. For a few hours of the night let your mind be at peace.
clean: What do you mean to do? Oh, Judah. Rest, sleep. For a few hours of the night let your mind be at peace.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: Peace? Love and peace. Do you think I don't long for them as much as you do? Where do you see them?
clean: Peace? Love and peace. Do you think I don't long for them as much as you do? Where do you see them?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Esther: If you had heard this man from Nazareth.
clean: If you had heard this man from Nazareth.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: Balthasar's word.
clean: Balthasar's word.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Esther: He is more than Balthasar's word. His voiced traveled with such a still purpose. It was more than a voice. A man more than a man. He said, 'Blessed are the merciful, for they shall obtain mercy. Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called the children of God.'
clean: He is more than Balthasar's word. His voiced traveled with such a still purpose. It was more than a voice. A man more than a man. He said, 'Blessed are the merciful, for they shall obtain mercy. Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called the children of God.'

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: Children of God? In that dead valley where we left them? I tell you, every man in Judea is unclean and will stay unclean until we've scoured off our bodies the crust and filth of being at the mercy of tyranny. No other life is possible except to wash this land clean.
clean: Children of God? In that dead valley where we left them? I tell you, every man in Judea is unclean and will stay unclean until we've scoured off our bodies the crust and filth of being at the mercy of tyranny. No other life is possible except to wash this land clean.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Esther: In blood?
clean: In blood?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: Yes, in blood!
clean: Yes, in blood!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Esther: I know there is a law in life. That blood begets more blood as dog begets dog. Death generates death as the vulture breeds the vulture. But the voice I heard today on the hill said, 'Love your enemy. Do good to those who spitefully use you.'
clean: I know there is a law in life. That blood begets more blood as dog begets dog. Death generates death as the vulture breeds the vulture. But the voice I heard today on the hill said, 'Love your enemy. Do good to those who spitefully use you.'

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: All who are born in this land hereafter can suffer as we have done.
clean: All who are born in this land hereafter can suffer as we have done.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Esther: As you make us do now! Are we to bear nothing together, even love?
clean: As you make us do now! Are we to bear nothing together, even love?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: I could hardly draw a breath without feeling you in my heart. But I know... that everything I do from this moment... bears greater pain than you have ever suffered. It is better not to love me.
clean: I could hardly draw a breath without feeling you in my heart. But I know... that everything I do from this moment... bears greater pain than you have ever suffered. It is better not to love me.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Esther: It was Judah Ben-Hur I loved. What has become of him? You seem to be now the very thing you set out to destroy, giving evil for evil. Hatred is turning you to stone. It's as though you had become Messala! [Judah looks at her, shocked] I've lost you, Judah.
clean: It was Judah Ben-Hur I loved. What has become of him? You seem to be now the very thing you set out to destroy, giving evil for evil. Hatred is turning you to stone. It's as though you had become Messala! I've lost you, Judah.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: This is where your search has brought you, Balthasar. He gave me water... and a heart to live. What has He done to merit this?
clean: This is where your search has brought you, Balthasar. He gave me water... and a heart to live. What has He done to merit this?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Balthasar: He has taken the world of our sins onto Himself. To this end He said He was born, in that stable, where I first saw Him. For this cause, He came into the world.
clean: He has taken the world of our sins onto Himself. To this end He said He was born, in that stable, where I first saw Him. For this cause, He came into the world.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: For this death?
clean: For this death?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Balthasar: For this beginning.
clean: For this beginning.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Miriam: [about Jesus] As though He were carrying in that cross the pain of the world.
clean: As though He were carrying in that cross the pain of the world.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Tirzah: So fearful, and yet why is it I'm not afraid anymore?
clean: So fearful, and yet why is it I'm not afraid anymore?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Esther: The shadow of a storm. [The sky goes dark while she, Miriam and Tirzah enter the cave] A strange darkness... but still day.
clean: The shadow of a storm. A strange darkness... but still day.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Miriam: His life is over.
clean: His life is over.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: Almost at the moment He died, I heard Him say, "Father, forgive them for they know not what they do."
clean: Almost at the moment He died, I heard Him say, "Father, forgive them for they know not what they do."

heading: Dialogue
raw: Esther: Even then.
clean: Even then.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judah: Even then. And I felt His voice take the sword out of my hand.
clean: Even then. And I felt His voice take the sword out of my hand.
